nameMeanings.name: Sanches
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Juan, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Adolfo, Alina, Arturo, Domingos, Guillermo.  Portuguese Joao, Amadeu, Marcio.
Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name   Sancho  .
Spanish: variant or altered form of Sánchez (see Sanchez ).

variants.name: Sancho
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Elena, Carlos, Fernando, Jacinto, Mario, Agustin, Ana, Dulce, Emilio, Enrique, Eugenio.

Spanish and Portuguese: from Sancho, a popular medieval personal name, which is probably from Latin Sanc(t)ius, a derivative of sanctus ‘holy’. The personal name was borne by a 9th-century Christian martyr of Cordova.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Barbosa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Francisco, Miguel, Luis, Pedro, Alfredo, Carlos, Ricardo, Angel, Armando.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo, Agostinho, Manoel, Serafim.  Italian Antonio, Marco, Angelo, Carmelo, Eliseo, Leonardo, Sal, Albertina, Cesario, Constantino, Fausto, Flavio.
Galician and Portuguese: topographic name from barba, a type of plant, + the suffix -osa, denoting a place rich in this plant.

variants.name: Barboza
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Juan, Ricardo, Javier, Jesus, Jorge, Luis, Alejandro, Jose Luis, Mario.  Portuguese Armanda, Joao, Marcio, Paulo.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa was born in 1125, in Spain, her father, Sancho Núñez de Celanova, was 55 and her mother, Sancha Henriques Infanta de Portugal, was 31. She had at least 7 sons and 5 daughters with Pedro Fernandes de Bragança.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa est née en 1125, à Espagne, son père, Sancho Núñez de Celanova, avait 55 ans et sa mère, Sancha Henriques Infanta de Portugal, avait 31 ans. Elle a eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles avec Pedro Fernandes de Bragança.

de.Tougues, Vila do Conde, Porto, Portugal: Tougues, Vila do Conde, Porto, Portugal
de.lifeSummary: Als Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa 1125 in Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sancho Núñez de Celanova 55 und ihre Mutter Sancha Henriques Infanta de Portugal 31. Sie hatte mit Pedro Fernandes de Bragança mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter.

ja.lifeSummary: Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosaが1125年にスペインで生まれた時，父Sancho Núñez de Celanovaは55歳，母Sancha Henriques Infanta de Portugalは31歳であった。彼女はPedro Fernandes de Bragançaとの間に少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa(이)가 1125년 스페인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sancho Núñez de Celanova(은)는 55세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sancha Henriques Infanta de Portugal(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 Pedro Fernandes de Bragança(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa родилась в 1125 г. в местности Испания, её отцу по имени Sancho Núñez de Celanova было 55 лет, а матери, которую звали Sancha Henriques Infanta de Portugal, 31. У неё было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей от Pedro Fernandes de Bragança.
